step_by_step_reasoning: On-premises camera/encoder streams live video to AWS Elemental MediaStore. MediaStore feeds Elemental MediaLive for distribution encoding, and archives video to S3. MediaLive streams to Elemental MediaPackage to package content for various viewer devices. MediaPackage routes via CloudFront to viewers. Separately, archived video in S3 is analyzed by Amazon Rekognition (for facial indexing) and Amazon Transcribe (for captions). AWS Systems Manager, triggered by Lambda, automates and controls the on-prem encoder equipment via APIs.

graph.name: Graham Media Group Modernized Broadcast with AWS Elemental and AI
graph.notes: Hybrid system automating traditional broadcast video workflows into digital live-streams and indexed video archives using AWS Elemental, S3, AI, and Systems Manager.

nodes.name: On-Prem Encoder
nodes.notes: Local broadcast cameras and hardware encoders.

nodes.name: Elemental MediaStore
nodes.notes: Ingests and stores live contribution stream.

nodes.name: Elemental MediaLive
nodes.notes: Encodes live video streams in real-time.

nodes.name: Elemental MediaPackage
nodes.notes: Formats and packages video streams for diverse end devices.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Long-term archive of all broadcasted media.

nodes.name: Rekognition
nodes.notes: Identifies on-screen talent for media indexing.

nodes.name: Systems Manager
nodes.notes: Controls and automates on-premises local encoders.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Fires automation APIs to control local station equipment.

nodes.name: Viewers
nodes.notes: Cell phone, tablet, and HDTV consumers.

edges.notes: Delivers live stream to end devices.
Invokes local encoder APIs to switch feeds/ads.
